description: Calculates potential cost savings from VM rightsizing by analyzing usage patterns. Identifies instances with high drift (>20%) and recommends optimal sizing based on P95 usage with 20% headroom.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE drift_metrics.combined_drift_score > 20
| STATS
    avg_cpu_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.cpu.drift_percent),
    avg_memory_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.memory.drift_percent),
    avg_combined_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.combined_drift_score),
    p95_cpu_pct = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.p95_percent),
    p95_memory_pct = AVG(resource_usage.memory.p95_percent),
    avg_cpu_cores_used = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_cores_used),
    avg_memory_gb_used = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_gb_used),
    total_cost_usd = SUM(cost_actual.total_cost_usd),
    exec_count = COUNT(*)
    BY metadata.team.keyword,
       vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword,
       vm_info.zone.keyword
| EVAL
    needs_optimization = CASE(
        avg_combined_drift > 40, "HIGH",
        avg_combined_drift > 25, "MEDIUM",
        "LOW"),
    recommended_cpu_cores = CEIL(avg_cpu_cores_used * 1.2),
    recommended_memory_gb = CEIL(avg_memory_gb_used * 1.2)
| SORT avg_combined_drift DESC
| LIMIT 10

description: Analyzes GCP VM resource usage patterns, identifies drift between requested and actual usage, and highlights over-provisioned instances. Returns CPU/memory usage, drift metrics, and efficiency scores.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| STATS
    avg_cpu_usage = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    p95_cpu_usage = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.p95_percent),
    avg_memory_usage = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_percent),
    p95_memory_usage = AVG(resource_usage.memory.p95_percent),
    avg_cpu_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.cpu.drift_percent),
    avg_memory_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.memory.drift_percent),
    avg_combined_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.combined_drift_score),
    avg_cpu_cores_used = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_cores_used),
    avg_memory_gb_used = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_gb_used),
    sample_count = COUNT(*)
    BY resource_name.keyword,
       vm_info.vm_id.keyword,
       vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword
| SORT avg_combined_drift DESC
| LIMIT 20

description: Compares different GCP VM machine types for cost and performance trade offs. Filters by minimum CPU and memory requirements and calculates efficiency scores.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-pricing-catalog
| WHERE region == "us-central1" 
  AND cpu_cores >= 4
  AND memory_gb >= 16
| EVAL 
    monthly_cost = cost_per_month_usd,
    efficiency_score = (cpu_cores + memory_gb) / cost_per_month_usd,
    cost_per_resource = cost_per_month_usd / (cpu_cores + memory_gb)
| SORT cost_per_month_usd ASC
| KEEP machine_type, machine_family, cpu_cores, memory_gb, 
       cost_per_hour_usd, cost_per_month_usd, 
       use_case, description, efficiency_score, cost_per_resource
| LIMIT 20

description: Analyzes VM sizing efficiency by comparing allocated resources (requested VM size) vs actual usage. Shows which VMs are over-provisioned based on P95 usage patterns. Useful for identifying rightsizing opportunities by team and region.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    p95_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.p95_percent),
    avg_memory_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_percent),
    p95_memory_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.p95_percent),
    avg_cpu_cores_used = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_cores_used),
    peak_cpu_cores_used = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.peak_cores_used),
    avg_memory_gb_used = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_gb_used),
    cpu_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.cpu.drift_percent),
    memory_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.memory.drift_percent),
    combined_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.combined_drift_score),
    sample_count = COUNT(*)
    BY vm_info.vm_id.keyword,
       resource_name.keyword,
       vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword,
       metadata.team.keyword,
       vm_info.zone.keyword
| SORT combined_drift DESC
| LIMIT 20

description: Compares what teams have ALLOCATED (VM sizes) vs what they ACTUALLY USE (real metrics). Calculates efficiency scores to identify which teams are over-provisioning the most. Lower efficiency score = more waste.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_cpu_used_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    p95_cpu_used_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.p95_percent),
    avg_memory_used_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_percent),
    p95_memory_used_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.p95_percent),
    avg_cpu_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.cpu.drift_percent),
    avg_memory_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.memory.drift_percent),
    avg_combined_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.combined_drift_score),
    common_vm_types = VALUES(vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword),
    executions = COUNT(*),
    total_cost_usd = SUM(cost_actual.total_cost_usd)
    BY metadata.team.keyword
| EVAL
    efficiency_score = 100 - avg_combined_drift
| SORT efficiency_score ASC
| LIMIT 20

description: Calculates potential cost savings from VM rightsizing by analyzing usage patterns. Identifies instances with high drift (>20%) and recommends optimal sizing based on P95 usage with 20% headroom.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_cpu = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    avg_memory = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_percent),
    avg_drift = AVG(drift_metrics.combined_drift_score),
    executions = COUNT(*),
    avg_cores_used = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_cores_used),
    avg_memory_gb_used = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_gb_used),
    total_cost_usd = SUM(cost_actual.total_cost_usd)
    BY metadata.workload_type.keyword,
       vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword,
       metadata.team.keyword
| SORT avg_drift DESC
| LIMIT 15

description: Analyzes historical execution data for specific workload types to show actual resource consumption patterns, including average, peak, and P95 usage. Helps recommend instance sizes based on real-world data.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    data_points = COUNT(*),
    avg_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    p95_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.p95_percent),
    max_cpu_percent = MAX(resource_usage.cpu.peak_percent),
    avg_memory_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_percent),
    p95_memory_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.p95_percent),
    max_memory_percent = MAX(resource_usage.memory.peak_percent),
    avg_duration_minutes = AVG(execution_time.duration_minutes),
    first_seen = MIN(execution_time.started_at),
    last_seen = MAX(execution_time.started_at)
    BY vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword,
       metadata.team.keyword,
       metadata.workload_type.keyword
| SORT data_points DESC
| LIMIT 20

description: Analyzes workload patterns filtered by team, environment, workload_type, or other labels. Returns label-specific resource usage patterns to answer: "What does MY team actually use for this workload type?"

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    data_points = COUNT(*),
    executions = COUNT(*),
    avg_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    p95_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.p95_percent),
    avg_memory_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_percent),
    p95_memory_percent = AVG(resource_usage.memory.p95_percent),
    common_machine_types = VALUES(vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword)
    BY metadata.team.keyword,
       metadata.environment.keyword,
       metadata.workload_type.keyword
| SORT data_points DESC
| LIMIT 20

description: Recommends GCP instance types based on CPU and memory requirements. Finds instances that meet minimum specs while optimizing for cost. Results should be filtered by the agent based on user's specific CPU/memory needs.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-vm-pricing
| EVAL
    cost_per_core_hour = pricing.price_per_hour_usd / pricing.cpu_cores,
    cost_per_gb_hour = pricing.price_per_hour_usd / pricing.memory_gb
| KEEP machine_type, cloud.region, machine_series,
       pricing.cpu_cores, pricing.memory_gb, 
       pricing.price_per_hour_usd, pricing.price_per_month_usd,
       cost_per_core_hour, cost_per_gb_hour
| SORT pricing.price_per_month_usd ASC
| LIMIT 50

description: Calculates cost projections based on recent usage trends. Uses 7-day historical data to project 30-day costs with trend analysis. Not ML-based forecasting (which requires Elasticsearch Cloud, not Serverless).

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE execution_time.started_at > NOW() - 30 days
| EVAL daily_bucket = DATE_TRUNC(1 day, execution_time.started_at)
| STATS
    daily_cost = SUM(cost_actual.total_cost_usd),
    daily_runs = COUNT(*)
    BY daily_bucket,
       metadata.team.keyword
| STATS
    avg_daily_cost = AVG(daily_cost),
    min_daily_cost = MIN(daily_cost),
    max_daily_cost = MAX(daily_cost),
    days_of_data = COUNT(*)
    BY metadata.team.keyword
| EVAL
    trend_per_day = (max_daily_cost - min_daily_cost) / days_of_data,
    projected_30_day_cost = avg_daily_cost * 30,
    projected_with_trend_30_day = (avg_daily_cost + (trend_per_day * 15)) * 30
| SORT projected_30_day_cost DESC
| LIMIT 10

description: Identifies VMs with unusual resource usage patterns detected by the ML job `vm-capacity-planning`. Queries real ML anomaly results from `.ml-anomalies-*` index. Automatically finds problems that deviate from learned baselines.

configuration.query:
FROM .ml-anomalies-*
| WHERE job_id == "vm-capacity-planning"
  AND result_type == "bucket"
  AND anomaly_score > 0
  AND is_interim == false
| EVAL 
    anomaly_date = DATE_FORMAT("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm", timestamp),
    severity_level = CASE(
        anomaly_score >= 75, "CRITICAL",
        anomaly_score >= 50, "HIGH",
        anomaly_score >= 25, "MEDIUM",
        "LOW"
    )
| SORT anomaly_score DESC
| KEEP anomaly_date, severity_level, anomaly_score, 
       event_count, bucket_span
| LIMIT 20

description: Analyzes 30-day CPU usage trends to predict when teams will hit 90% capacity. Calculates growth rates and provides proactive resize recommendations based on real metrics.

configuration.query:
FROM ml-predictions-growth-summary
| EVAL
    current_utilization_percent = (current_cores_used / current_vm_capacity) * 100,
    urgency = CASE(
        weeks_until_90_percent_capacity <= 4, "URGENT",
        weeks_until_90_percent_capacity <= 8, "SOON",
        weeks_until_90_percent_capacity <= 12, "PLAN",
        "WATCH")
| KEEP @timestamp, team, workload_type, urgency, recommendation,
       current_cores_used, current_vm_capacity, current_utilization_percent,
       growth_rate_percent_per_week, growth_rate_percent_per_month,
       weeks_until_90_percent_capacity, confidence, r_squared
| SORT growth_rate_percent_per_week DESC
| LIMIT 10

description: Identifies workloads within teams that have unusual resource patterns. Classifies workloads as HEAVY_USER (>5 cores avg), LIGHT_USER (<1 core avg), or NORMAL. Results are sorted by team to make it easy to spot outliers within each team's context.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_cpu_cores = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_cores_used),
    peak_cpu_cores = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.peak_cores_used),
    avg_memory_gb = AVG(resource_usage.memory.avg_gb_used),
    avg_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    execution_count = COUNT(*),
    sample_machine = VALUES(vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword)
    BY metadata.team.keyword
| EVAL
    anomaly_score = CASE(
        avg_cpu_cores > 7, 100,
        avg_cpu_cores > 5, 75,
        avg_cpu_cores > 3, 50,
        avg_cpu_cores < 0.5, 75,
        avg_cpu_cores < 1, 50,
        25),
    anomaly_level = CASE(
        avg_cpu_cores > 7 OR avg_cpu_cores < 0.5, "CRITICAL",
        avg_cpu_cores > 5 OR avg_cpu_cores < 1, "HIGH",
        avg_cpu_cores > 3, "MEDIUM",
        "NORMAL"),
    resource_pattern = CASE(
        avg_cpu_cores > 5, "HEAVY_USER",
        avg_cpu_cores < 1, "LIGHT_USER",
        "NORMAL")
| WHERE anomaly_score > 25
| KEEP metadata.team.keyword, anomaly_level, resource_pattern,
       avg_cpu_cores, peak_cpu_cores, avg_cpu_percent, avg_memory_gb,
       execution_count, sample_machine
| SORT avg_cpu_cores DESC
| LIMIT 50

description: Calculates cost per instance run by combining uptime data with pricing. Shows which instances cost the most and their efficiency (high CPU usage = efficient, low = wasteful). Ideal for analyzing short-lived CI/CD instances.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-resource-executions-*
| WHERE metadata.team IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_cpu_percent = AVG(resource_usage.cpu.avg_percent),
    avg_duration_minutes = AVG(execution_time.duration_minutes),
    total_cost_usd = SUM(cost_actual.total_cost_usd),
    runs = COUNT(*)
    BY resource_name.keyword,
       vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword,
       metadata.team.keyword
| EVAL
    cost_per_run = total_cost_usd / runs,
    cost_efficiency = CASE(
        avg_cpu_percent > 70, "Efficient",
        avg_cpu_percent > 40, "Moderate",
        "Wasteful")
| KEEP resource_name.keyword, metadata.team.keyword, vm_info.vm_type_actual.keyword,
       runs, avg_duration_minutes, avg_cpu_percent,
       total_cost_usd, cost_per_run, cost_efficiency
| SORT total_cost_usd DESC
| LIMIT 50

description: Retrieves current cost and technical specifications for any VM instance type from the GCP pricing catalog. Shows pricing across all machine types in the catalog.

configuration.query:
FROM gcp-pricing-catalog
| KEEP machine_type, machine_family, region, cpu_cores, memory_gb, 
       cost_per_hour_usd, cost_per_month_usd, 
       cost_per_core_hour, cost_per_gb_hour, 
       description, use_case
| SORT cost_per_month_usd ASC
| LIMIT 30
